data.title: Atlantic Lithium wins Ghana parliament's approval for Africa's only U.S.-bound lithium project
data.description:
Ghana's parliament has ratified Atlantic Lithium’s (ALLIF) mining lease for the flagship Ewoyaa lithium mine and processing plant on terms more favorable than those sought under a previous government, MiningWeekly.com reported Friday.

Atlantic Lithium (ALLIF) said the Ewoyaa mining lease is the first to be granted and ratified for the mining of lithium in Ghana.

Under the lease terms, which encompass a new royalty framework for lithium and gold, Atlantic Lithium (ALLIF) said it has the exclusive rights to carry out mining and commercial production activities over the mining lease area for an initial 15-year period, renewable in accordance with Ghanaian legislation.

Atlantic Lithium (ALLIF) said it will pay royalties on a scale linked to the spodumene spot prices, ranging from 5% when the price for concentrate is below $1,500/ton to 12% if the price tops $3,200/ton, a structure that replaces a flat 10% rate Ghana had sought when the company first applied for a permit more than two years ago.

The Ewoyaa mine will be Africa’s only U.S.-bound lithium project, with all others backed by Chinese funding, and is expected to produce 3.6M tons of spodumene concentrate over 12 years, making it Africa's third-largest lithium project under development, according to the company.

data.title: Rubrik started with Buy rating at BTIG
data.description:
• Rubrik (RBRK) was in focus on Friday as investment firm BTIG started coverage on the cybersecurity firm with a Buy rating and $64 price target.
  • “In our view, several durable tailwinds should support elevated revenue growth over the next few years,” analyst Gray Powell wrote in a note to clients.
  • Powell continued: “To start, ransomware and wiper attack volumes have risen sharply in recent years, and we expect that trend to continue as threat actors increasingly leverage AI. As a result, backup and data protection are becoming a more critical part of enterprise security architecture.”
  • Powell also noted that Rubrik was “frequently described” by industry contacts “as the most aggressive and innovative vendor in the space, which we believe is reflected in the company’s meaningful share gains over the past three years.”
  • Additionally, Powell believes artificial intelligence should be a tailwind for the company, not a headwind.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500, Dow fall even as Netanyahu eases concerns about the Iran conflict
data.description:
Wall Street fell on Friday even as Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said the country is helping to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

S&P 500 (SP500) -0.2%, the Dow (DJI) -0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.6%.

Oil prices slipped on the news that Netanyahu said Iran no longer has the capacity to enrich uranium or make ballistic missiles.

Treasury yields edged higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.33%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) climbed 9 basis points to 3.9%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) added 5 basis points to 4.89%.

There are no major economic reports slated for the day.

data.title: Apple's Tim Cook meets Chinese minister, discusses global supply chain
data.description:
Apple's (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook met with Chinese Minister of Commerce Wang Wentao on Friday, according to a video clip released on the WeChat account of China's Ministry of Commerce.

Cook kicked off his China tour on Wednesday, where he visited an Apple Store in Chengdu, the capital of southwestern Sichuan province, as part of the company’s 50th anniversary celebrations.

In the meeting with Wang, key topics included the global supply chain and China's efforts to boost domestic consumption. Cook reaffirmed the importance of the Chinese market and expressed appreciation for recent consumption incentive policies, according to several media reports.

"China continues to be the vast majority of our supply chain in the world, with 80 of our top 100 suppliers in China," said Cook. "We are very much focused on high-quality development."

"We had good business over the last several months in China; we are very appreciative of the government incentives that are stimulating consumption in the country," Cook added.

According to Counterpoint Research, Apple's iPhone sales in China surged 23% year-over-year in the first nine weeks of 2026.

Cook's visit comes ahead of the China Development Forum 2026, which will be held from March 22-23. He previously visited China in October, where he met government officials and pledged to increase investment in China amid the U.S.-China trade tussle.

In January, China’s Vice Premier He Lifeng reportedly met with Cook and several other top global executives at the World Economic Forum in Davos. Earlier this week, Cook squashed rumors that he was considering retiring from the company he has led since 2011.

Apple's Chief Operating Officer Sabih Khan reportedly met several supply chain partners, including Foxconn, earlier this week.

The Chinese ruling party’s flagship newspaper People’s Daily said on Tuesday that Apple needs to ease App Store restrictions and fix alleged “monopolistic” practices.

Last week, Apple said it would lower the commission fees it collects from its App Store in mainland China starting from Sunday, marking a win for Chinese app developers, following discussions with the country's regulator.

data.title: Arm in spotlight as HSBC upgrades to Buy on 'game-changing' CPU narrative
data.description:
Arm Holdings (ARM) shares rose 4% in premarket trading on Friday after HSBC upgraded the British semiconductor intellectual property company to Buy, citing a “game-changing” artificial intelligence-related narrative. 

“We believe Arm is now firmly in the middle of a transition from being a smartphone dependent semi-IP play, into a major AI server CPU beneficiary that remains undervalued by the market,” analyst Frank Lee wrote in a note to clients. “As highlighted in our previous research, agentic AI is expanding the TAM for server CPUs which should drive server unit growth upside. All major hyperscalers also have Arm-based server CPUs and are moving to both its v9 architecture as well as Neoverse Compute Subsystems (CSS), doubling its royalty revenue per chip. We also believe Arm will enter the merchant CPU business which will not only significantly boost its revenue and earnings potential but could also lead to a potential re-rating.”

In addition to the upgrade, Lee raised his price target on Arm to $205 from $90.

Delving deeper, Lee said he believes royalties from server CPUs could be as big as the current overall company revenue by 2030 (perhaps to the tune of around $4B), given how in demand server CPUs are for AI-related work.

“We expect industry CPU shipments to grow by 20% in 2026e and 21% y-o-y in 2027e, compared to an average growth of 2% y-o-y during 2021-25,” Lee added. “Its customers are moving to adopt the Arm v9 architecture as well as CSS, doubling the royalty revenue per chip. Additionally, there is an ongoing increase in server CPU core counts for performance enhancement and power efficiency. Driven by these factors, we believe Arm’s server CPU royalty revenue could grow at a CAGR of 76% from FY2026-31.” 

Lastly, considering Arm may be working on its own CPU (when looking at rising R&D costs), Lee said this could be a game-changer for Arm.

“We believe this significantly raises the revenue per chip, from a royalty of $36-132 to a chip ASP of $1,000 per CPU, which can materially change Arm’s fundamentals and economics,” Lee added. 

Arm is set to host its Arm Everywhere event on March 24, where it may give updates on its work.

data.title: U.S. likely to send ground forces to Iran soon, conflict to intensify, Woo says—CNBC interview
data.description:
The U.S. ground forces will likely be deployed to Iran within a week despite President Trump’s recent reassurances that boots on the ground may not be necessary, David Woo, CEO of David Woo Unbound, said. 

The analyst, speaking in an interview with CNBC, believes an amphibious assault on the Iranian coastline near Bandar Abbas is “probably the only course of action that’s available to Trump in the coming days” to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Woo expressed skepticism about the administration’s optimistic messaging, noting that throughout the nineteen-day conflict,

"Trump has been trying to talk up the stock market and talk down oil prices."

He cautioned investors not to take the president’s statements at face value, suggesting the administration’s interest lies in maintaining political capital to continue the war effort.

Despite recent bunker buster bomb strikes along the coastline near Bandar Abbas, Woo believes air power alone will not be sufficient to neutralize Iranian positions. 

“The terrain makes it very difficult because, again, Bandar Abbas backs onto these very, very high ridges that provide excellent natural defenses for Iranian troops and firing positions,” he explained. If airstrikes fail to stop Iranian forces within the next week, he believes ground forces will become inevitable.

The conflict shows no signs of winding down, according to the analyst, who pointed to Iran’s continued missile capabilities as evidence.

Following a speech by Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, Iran launched overnight attacks on Israel, demonstrating that “there’s no evidence so far that they’re running out of missiles.” This suggests investors should be pricing in a longer conflict than many currently anticipate.

From an investment perspective, Woo is positioned short on the Nasdaq, citing concerns that the AI trade will suffer as companies face higher energy costs and potential excuses to reduce capital expenditure.

“AI also is very, very energy-intensive. So with energy prices at this level, it’s gonna be more difficult for a lot of these companies to go ahead and actually meet their CapEx spending target for this year,” he noted.

Woo remains long on oil and expects prices to climb higher as the conflict intensifies. He also favors the five-thirty steepener, particularly the five-year part of the Treasury curve, arguing that rising oil prices will actually be disinflationary for core inflation as Americans have less money to spend after paying for gasoline.

His main thesis is clear: “The war is gonna get ugly before it ends.”

data.title: Bearish sentiment tops 50% as AAII survey signals sharp investor shift
data.description:
Investor pessimism has surged to its highest level in recent weeks, according to the latest American Association of Individual Investors sentiment survey, which now shows a decisive shift toward a bearish outlook.

For the week ending March 18, more than half, 52% of respondents to be exact, said they hold a bearish outlook over the next six months, marking a notable increase in negative sentiment among individual investors. 

Bullish sentiment remained relatively subdued, with just 30.4% of participants anticipating market gains, while 17.6% expressed a neutral stance. The data underscores a growing sense of caution as market participants reassess risk amid heightened uncertainty fueled by the growing conflict in the Middle East. 

The shift is particularly striking when compared to late February readings. In the week ending February 25—prior to the escalation of geopolitical tensions stemming from the Iran-U.S. war—only 39.8% of surveyed investors identified as bearish. The sharp rise in pessimism since then highlights how quickly sentiment has deteriorated in response to evolving global developments. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).
